blog.title: What Actually Protects Your Home?
blog.description: A dinner conversation reveals why paying off your mortgage faster isn't always the safest strategy — and what actually protects your home when life goes wrong.

nftMetadata.description: AI generated artwork based on the prompt: "Soft pastel comic-style illustration. A modern apartment interior with a subtle split scene: on one side, money is visually embedded inside the walls of the home (like faint glowing shapes behind the wall), on the other side, a woman calmly holds a small stack of cash or a bank card in her hand. The contrast shows inaccessible wealth versus liquid savings. Minimalist, clean composition, light colors, friendly educational tone."

prevBlog.title: How to invest your money without predicting markets
prevBlog.description: A hands-on guide to ETF diversification using risk budgets, with interactive tools to find your own allocation.

nftMetadata.description: AI generated artwork based on the prompt: "Minimalist comic-style illustration with soft pastel colors. A confident professional woman looking at a map with several routes connecting two cities. Some routes look risky and winding, others slow and safe, while a balanced route combines multiple paths. The mood is thoughtful and strategic, symbolizing smart planning under uncertainty. Clean educational illustration style, light colors, simple shapes, white background."

nextBlog.title: My take on a housing podcast: Rent vs. Buy Is Not One Decision
nextBlog.description: The podcast argument against buying a home is strongest for young mobile professionals, but much weaker once you distinguish between different households and the very different risks they face.

nftMetadata.name: AI Generated Art #1778004598170
nftMetadata.description:
AI generated artwork based on the prompt: "A modern comic-style illustration of a person sitting at home listening to a podcast on headphones, with a smartphone showing a podcast interface.
From the phone, abstract visual elements emerge: charts, houses, arrows — initially simple and clean — but gradually branching into more complex paths representing different life situations.
The listener looks thoughtful, realizing the idea is more nuanced than it first appeared.
Style: warm, friendly, slightly stylized editorial illustration, clean lines, soft gradients, contemporary European interior."
